create_surface_based_element: Create surface-based elements (floors, ceilings). Pass a JSON array of creation specs: [{category, boundaryPoints:[{x,y,z}], typeName, levelId|levelName, ...}].
create_line_based_element: Create line-based elements (walls, beams). Pass a JSON array of specs: [{category, locationLine:{p0:{x,y,z}, p1:{x,y,z}, pMid?:{x,y,z}}, typeId?, height?, baseLevel?, baseOffset?}]. Add pMid to make a curved (arc) wall/beam. Coordinates in mm.
create_point_based_element: Create point-based elements (columns, furniture). Pass a JSON array of creation specs: [{category, location:{x,y,z}, typeName, levelId|levelName, rotation?, ...}].
create_floor: Create an architectural floor from a boundary (or a room), optionally with holes. Provide boundaryPoints OR roomId.
change_element_type: Change the type of one or more elements to a target type specified by ID or name.
create_grid: Create a grid system (X and/or Y grids by count + spacing), or rename/delete an existing grid. action=create|rename|delete. Spacing/extent values are in mm.
create_dimensions: Create dimension annotations in the active view. Pass a JSON array of dimension specs. Element mode: [{viewId, referenceIds:[...], linePoint:{x,y,z}, dimensionStyleId?}]. Point-to-point mode: [{viewId, startPoint:{x,y,z}, endPoint:{x,y,z}, linePoint?, dimensionStyleId?}]. dimensionStyleId is honored in both modes.
color_elements: Color the active view's elements of a category by grouping them on a parameter value, or reset (clear) those color overrides. action=color|reset. Operates on the ACTIVE model view (not a sheet).
export_to_excel: Export element data from a Revit category to an Excel file.
export_room_data: Export room data including area, perimeter, level, and bounding elements.
create_array: Create a linear or radial array. Default builds a real associative Revit ArrayElement (editable count); set associative=false for loose copies. linear uses spacingX/Y/Z (mm); radial uses centerX/Y (mm) and totalAngle.
create_color_legend: Color elements by parameter value and optionally create a legend view.
create_filled_region: Create a filled region in a view from a closed boundary, optionally with holes (inner loops).
create_structural_framing_system: Create a beam system on a level over a rectangular area. Default builds a real associative Revit BeamSystem (editable layout); set associative=false for loose independent beams.
create_text_note: Create text notes in a view. Pass a JSON array: [{text, position:{x,y,z}, viewId?, textNoteTypeId?, width?, horizontalAlignment?, verticalAlignment?, rotation?, leader?}]. rotation is degrees; leader is left|right|leftArc|rightArc.
create_revision: List, create, update, or assign revisions to sheets. action=list|create|set|add_to_sheets. 'set' updates an existing revision (needs revisionId).
import_from_excel: Import parameter values from an Excel file into Revit elements.
export_elements_data: Export element data by category as JSON or CSV. Supports parameter filtering and an optional parameter-based include filter.
export_families: Export loaded families as .rfa files into a target directory.
export_schedule: Export schedule to CSV/TSV or JSON
batch_export: Export views/sheets to DWG, DXF, DGN, PDF, or image (PNG) formats.
import_table: Import a CSV/TSV file as a formatted table in a drafting or legend view.
get_element_parameters: Get all parameters of specific elements by their Revit element IDs.
ai_element_filter: Query elements by category, element class, family symbol, bounding box, or level. Filters combine with AND (default) or OR, and the whole set can be inverted (NOT). Supports type and instance filtering. For parameter-VALUE filtering use filter_by_parameter_value.
get_selected_elements: Get currently selected elements in Revit.
get_elements_by_unique_id: Resolve Revit UniqueId strings to ElementId records for cross-app workflows.
operate_element: Select, highlight, isolate, hide, or zoom to elements. Actions: select, selectionbox, setcolor, settransparency, hide, temphide, isolate, unhide, resetisolate, delete.
copy_elements: Copy elements with optional mm offset. Can target a different view (sourceViewId+targetViewId) or another OPEN document (targetDocumentTitle).
delete_selection: Delete a saved selection filter by name
find_undimensioned_elements: Find elements not referenced by dimensions
find_untagged_elements: Find elements without tags in a view
match_element_properties: Copy parameter values from one source element to one or more target elements.
measure_between_elements: Measure distance between two elements or two points in mm. Provide either elementId1/elementId2, or point1/point2 (as JSON arrays [x,y,z]).
renumber_elements: Renumber rooms/doors/windows by location or name. Writes into the specified parameter; supports prefix/suffix and start/increment.
save_selection: Save element selection as named filter
section_box_from_selection: Create a 3D section box from selected elements
set_element_phase: Assign created/demolished phase to elements. Pass a JSON array of requests: [{elementId, phaseCreatedId?, phaseDemolishedId?}].
set_element_workset: Move elements to a different workset. Pass a JSON array of requests: [{elementId, worksetId|worksetName}].
get_elements_in_spatial_volume: Find elements within a 3D bounding box or room volume. volumeType=room uses volumeIds; volumeType=custom uses customMinX..customMaxZ.
get_linked_elements: Query elements from linked Revit models with optional filtering. parameterNames is additive — without it only basic fields are returned.
get_room_openings: Get doors/windows adjacent to rooms with dimensions. Filter by roomIds, roomNumbers, or levelName.
modify_element: Move, rotate, mirror, or copy elements. Vectors are {"x":mm,"y":mm,"z":mm} JSON objects. move needs translation; rotate needs rotationCenter + rotationAngle (degrees) and optionally rotationAxis (default Z); mirror needs mirrorPlaneOrigin + mirrorPlaneNormal; copy needs copyOffset.
push_to_powerbi: Export element parameters or existing schedules to CSV files in a local/OneDrive folder for Power BI refresh. Two modes: (1) categories + parameterNames -> one elements.csv; (2) scheduleIds -> one schedule_<Name>.csv per schedule. Scope can be limited to active view or current selection. Defaults to OneDrive GPA Ingegneria Srl\\RevitCortex\\<DocumentName>\\.
select_from_powerbi: Selects/zooms/isolates elements in the active Revit view from a Power BI drillthrough. Action: select | highlight | isolate.
push_table_to_powerbi: Writes an arbitrary table (headers + rows) to a CSV in the RevitCortex/OneDrive folder for Power BI. Use when the data was computed by you (LLM analysis, multi-doc aggregation, etc.) and is NOT a direct dump of Revit elements. Pass headers as a JSON array of strings; pass rows as a JSON array where each item is either an array (positional) or an object keyed by header. The file lives next to push_to_powerbi outputs and can be picked up by the same Power BI folder source.
pbi_check_auth: Reports the user's Power BI sign-in state. With signIn=true, starts an MSAL device-code flow inside Revit (a TaskDialog will show the URL + code; the user finishes login in their browser). Token is cached DPAPI-encrypted in %LOCALAPPDATA%\\.revitcortex\\msal_cache.bin.
pbi_list_workspaces: Lists Power BI workspaces (groups) accessible to the signed-in user. Read-only.
pbi_list_datasets: Lists push datasets in a Power BI workspace. Useful for finding an existing RevitCortex dataset before publishing. Read-only.
pbi_create_dataset: Creates a RevitCortex push dataset in a Power BI workspace. Idempotent: if a dataset with the same name already exists, returns its id without creating a duplicate. Default tables: Metadata, Elements, Selection.
pbi_publish_elements: Publishes Revit model elements to a Power BI push dataset (Elements table). workspaceId and datasetId can be omitted if a ProjectBinding was saved by a previous successful publish for this document. Supports replace (full snapshot, clears old rows), append (keeps old rows), and create (auto-create dataset if missing then replace). Snapshot runs on the Revit main thread; HTTP publish runs in the background.
pbi_publish_schedules: Publishes Revit schedules to the Power BI Schedules table in long-form (one row per cell). workspaceId and datasetId can be omitted if a ProjectBinding was saved by a previous successful publish for this document. Supports replace and append modes. Snapshot runs on the Revit main thread; HTTP publish runs in the background.
pbi_sign_out: Signs out of Power BI by revoking all cached MSAL tokens. After this call pbi_check_auth(signIn=false) returns signedIn=false. The previously signed-in account is reported in the response. Use pbi_check_auth(signIn=true) to re-authenticate.
pbi_get_binding: Returns the Power BI ProjectBinding stored for the active Revit document (workspaceId, datasetId, datasetName, docKey, updatedAt). Returns bound=false with a tip if no binding exists yet. Read-only — call this to verify which workspace/dataset a document is linked to before publishing.
pbi_publish_selection: Publishes the current Revit selection to the Power BI Selection table (one row per selected element). Each call replaces the previous snapshot (DELETE then POST). workspaceId and datasetId can be omitted when a ProjectBinding was saved by a previous publish for this document. Returns rowCount=0 with a warning if nothing is selected and clearIfEmpty is false.
pbi_query: Executes a DAX query against the bound Power BI dataset and selects matching elements in Revit. Use template params (category, level, parameterName+parameterValue, exportRunId) for common filters, or supply a raw daxQuery for advanced queries. action='isolate' temporarily isolates the elements instead of selecting. workspaceId and datasetId can be omitted when a ProjectBinding exists. Returns elementCount=0 with a warning when no elements match.
import_from_powerbi: Reads a previously-exported (or hand-edited) Power BI CSV and writes parameter values back to Revit elements. Identifies elements via the ElementId column. Built-in fields and read-only parameters are skipped. Defaults to dryRun=true so callers can preview first.
ifc_get_capabilities: Detect IFC version support and revit-ifc add-in presence
ifc_validate_request: Validate IFC file path, extension, and schema version.
ifc_link: Link an IFC file into the active document (creates a .ifc.RVT sidecar file managed by Revit).
ifc_reload_link: Reload an existing IFC link, optionally from a new file.
ifc_open_or_import: Open or import an IFC file as a native Revit project (actions: open | import).
ifc_export_basic: Export the active document to IFC. First-class flags cover the common options; use overrides for any other IFCExportOptions key.
ifc_export_with_configuration: Export using a named configuration (built-in or custom) with optional key/value overrides.
ifc_get_export_configuration: Get full details of a specific export configuration by name.
ifc_set_family_mapping_file: Set the family mapping file used by subsequent IFC exports.
ifc_analyze_rebuildability: Analyze IFC DirectShapes and score feasibility of rebuilding them as native Revit elements.
ifc_list_rebuild_candidates: List elements above a rebuild confidence threshold.
ifc_rebuild_walls: Rebuild native walls from IFC DirectShapes. dryRun defaults to true.
ifc_rebuild_floors: Rebuild native floors from IFC DirectShapes. dryRun defaults to true.
ifc_rebuild_roofs: Rebuild native roofs from IFC DirectShapes. dryRun defaults to true.
ifc_rebuild_structural_members: Rebuild columns and beams from IFC DirectShapes. dryRun defaults to true.
ifc_rebuild_openings: Cut openings in rebuilt walls/floors based on IFC opening DirectShapes.
ifc_rebuild_family_instances: Place family instances (doors, windows, furniture) from IFC DirectShapes.
ifc_compare_original_vs_rebuilt: Compare volume/geometry between the original DirectShape and its native rebuild.
ifc_tag_unreconstructable_elements: Tag IFC DirectShapes that cannot be rebuilt by writing a marker parameter.
cross_app_selection: Symmetric Revit↔Navis selection bridge. mode=export → emit CortexElementRefs from current Revit selection (host + linked). mode=import → consume CortexElementRefs and select/isolate them via show_cross_model_elements composition. Resolution priority: revitUniqueId → ifcGuid → revitElementId.
add_linked_file: Adds a new Revit linked file from a file path and optionally places an instance at the given position.
align_link_to_host: Aligns a link instance to the host project's internal origin, shared coordinates, or project base point.
get_link_transform: Returns the full transform of a linked file instance.
get_linked_file_instances: Lists all linked Revit files grouped by type, with transforms and load status.
get_coordination_models: Read-only listing of Autodesk Revit Coordination Models with type metadata and optional instances.
get_selected_linked_elements: Returns info about currently selected link instances.
highlight_linked_element: Highlights an element inside a linked model with an optional section box.
show_cross_model_elements: Select host elements plus elements in linked Revit models. Two strategies for visibility: (a) default — create red DirectShape markers in the host doc around each linked element's bounding box (synchronous, transactional, robust); (b) usePostCommandIsolate=true — use Revit's native IsolateElements via PostCommand after SetReferences (canonical Revit API pattern, but asynchronous: tool returns before isolate completes, and cannot be combined with section box / overrides in the same call).
manage_links: List, reload, reload-from-path, unload, or remove linked files. To add a NEW link use add_linked_file instead.
move_link_instance: Moves a linked file instance. mode=delta applies (x,y,z) as an offset; mode=absolute places the origin at (x,y,z). Values are in mm.
pin_unpin_link_instance: Pins or unpins linked file instances.
reload_linked_file_from: Reloads a linked Revit file from a different file path.
cad_link_cleanup: Analyze and clean up imported/linked CAD files. action=list|delete.
get_materials: List all materials in the active Revit document.
create_material: Create a new material in the Revit project.
get_compound_structure: Get wall/floor/roof/ceiling layer structure by type ID or name.
set_compound_structure: Modify compound structure on a wall/floor/roof/ceiling type. action=replace|add|remove|modify|set_wrapping. set_wrapping sets openingWrapping (none|exterior|interior|both), endCap (none|exterior|interior), and per-layer layerWrapping.
get_material_properties: Get detailed material properties (physical, thermal, appearance) by material ID or name.
get_material_quantities: Calculate material area and volume across elements, optionally filtered by category or restricted to the current selection.
delete_material: Delete a material from the project by ID or name.
duplicate_material: Duplicate an existing material with a new name.
duplicate_family_type: Duplicate a loadable family type with a new name and optional parameter overrides.
say_hello: Test MCP connection to RevitCortex. Displays a greeting in Revit.
get_project_info: Get project name, address, levels, phases, worksets, and links from the active Revit document.
set_project_info: Set editable Project Information fields. Only the fields you pass are changed; others are left untouched.
get_cache_stats: Return diagnostic hit/miss telemetry from the plugin-side tool-result cache.
clear_cache: Clear every entry from the plugin-side tool-result cache.
set_element_parameters: Set parameter values on one or more elements. Pass requests as a JSON-encoded array string. UNITS: a numeric value on a length/area/etc. parameter is written in Revit internal units (feet) — to set a display value, pass a STRING WITH A UNIT (e.g. "3000 mm", "3 m") and Revit parses it unit- and locale-aware. A null value clears the parameter. Note: type-level parameters (e.g. structural thickness on floors) cannot be set on instances — identify instance vs type params with get_element_parameters first, then set type params on the type ElementId instead.
bulk_modify_parameter_values: Bulk modify parameter values across elements by category. Supports set, find-and-replace, and other operations.
filter_by_parameter_value: Filter elements by one parameter condition, or several combined with AND/OR via the conditions array. Conditions: equals, not_equals, contains, not_contains, begins_with, ends_with, greater_than, less_than, is_empty, is_not_empty.
sync_csv_parameters: Synchronize parameter values from CSV data into Revit elements.
add_prefix_suffix: Add a prefix and/or suffix to parameter values across the model or a selection.
clear_parameter_values: Clear parameter values on elements by category or scope
add_shared_parameter: Add a shared parameter to project categories. The data type of a newly created definition is honored (a typed shared parameter, not always Text).
manage_project_parameters: Manage project parameters. Actions: list | create | delete | modify | set_group | set_binding_type | rename. 'delete' now correctly removes non-shared parameters too (works around Revit bug REVIT-136670 by deleting the ParameterElement) and verifies removal. 'modify' supports add/remove/replace on category bindings. 'set_group' bulk-changes the group assignment. 'set_binding_type' toggles a parameter between instance and type. 'rename' is NOT supported by the Revit API for bound project parameters and returns guidance (only global parameters can be renamed).
transfer_parameters: Copy parameter values from source element to one or more target elements.
manage_global_parameters: Manage global parameters (project-level named values). Actions: list | get | create | set | delete | rename | set_formula | move_up | move_down | sort. Unlike project/shared parameters, global parameters CAN be renamed. 'set_formula' drives the value by a formula (empty string clears it). 'move_up'/'move_down' reorder within the group; 'sort' orders ascending/descending.
get_warnings: Get model warnings from the active Revit document.
get_phases: List all project phases in the active Revit document.
get_worksets: List all worksets in the active Revit document.
manage_worksets: Create, rename, delete, or set the active workset (workshared models only). To LIST worksets use get_worksets.
load_family: Load a family into the Revit project.
get_available_family_types: List available family types in the Revit project.
analyze_model_statistics: Analyze element counts by category in the active Revit document.
check_model_health: Run a model health check and return a health score.
audit_families: Audit families in the Revit project. Lists loadable (.rfa) families by default; set includeSystemFamilies=true to also list system-family types (wall/floor/roof/ceiling types, including Foundation Slab).
manage_phase_filters: List, set, or create Revit Phase Filters. Actions: list | set | create. The 'set' action changes one presentation (New | Demolished | Existing | Temporary) on one filter and preserves the other three. Presentations: None | ByCategory | Overridden | NotDisplayed.
purge_unused: Purge unused families/types and materials, and optionally unreferenced view templates and view filters, from the project.
clash_detection: Detect clashes between two element categories. Uses true solid-geometry intersection by default (fewer false positives than bounding boxes).
workflow_model_audit: Run a complete model audit workflow.
create_level: Create, edit, rename, or delete a level. action=create|set|rename|delete. For set/rename/delete identify the level by levelId or name.
create_room: Create a new room in the Revit project.
delete_element: Delete elements from the Revit project.
duplicate_system_type: Duplicate, rename, or delete a system type (wall, floor, roof, ceiling). action=duplicate|rename|delete.
batch_rename: Batch rename elements or system types in the Revit project. Supports both loadable-family elements and system types (wall/floor/ceiling/roof types).
rename_families: Rename loaded families (and optionally their types) with find/replace, prefix, or suffix operations.
tag_rooms: Tag rooms in the active view. Operates on the active view only — activate the correct view first.
tag_walls: Tag walls at their midpoints in the active view. Operates on the active view only. Tags all walls by default, or a subset via wallIds.
wipe_empty_tags: Find and remove empty or orphaned tags
set_material_properties: Set identity, appearance, product info, and asset assignments on Revit materials. Each request is a FLAT object keyed by materialId plus any of: name, color (#RRGGBB), transparency, shininess, smoothness, materialClass, materialCategory, description, manufacturer, model, url, cost, mark, keynote, comments, appearanceAssetId, structuralAssetId, thermalAssetId (asset ids: 0/-1 clears).
modify_schedule: Modify schedule fields, sorting, filters, or rename the schedule. Supported actions: add_field, remove_field, set_sorting, clear_sorting, set_filter, clear_filter, rename.
send_code_to_revit: LAST RESORT ONLY — execute custom C# code in Revit. Always prefer dedicated tools (batch_rename, set_element_parameters, export_to_excel, etc.) over this tool. Use only when no dedicated tool covers the operation. Scripts are saved to ~/.revitcortex/scripts/ and require user confirmation before execution.
workflow_clash_review: Detect clashes between two categories and create a 3D section-boxed view for visual review.
workflow_data_roundtrip: Export parameters to Excel for external editing, then re-import once the file has been saved.
workflow_room_documentation: Auto-generate callout views (and optionally sections) for every room on a level.
workflow_sheet_set: Auto-create a set of sheets with title blocks from a definition list: [{number, name, viewIds?}].
get_shared_parameters: List all project parameters with their bindings and categories, optionally filtered by category.
lines_per_view_count: Count detail and/or model lines per view for performance auditing. Always set threshold >= 20 on large models; defaults to a guarded 100-view scan with a time budget.
list_family_sizes: List loaded families with type/instance counts and, when includeSize=true, the family file size in KB measured by exporting each family to a temp file. Disabled by default because exporting is slow on models with many families.
list_schedulable_fields: Discover available schedulable fields for a category.
manage_project_units: Get or set project units (length, area, volume, angle, etc.). Actions: get, set, list_valid_units.
manage_additional_settings: Manage Additional Settings (Manage tab): line styles, line weights, line patterns, fill patterns, halftone/underlay.
create_view: Create a new view in Revit: floor plan, ceiling plan, section, elevation, drafting, or 3D view.
duplicate_view: Duplicate an existing view in Revit.
get_current_view_info: Get information about the currently active view in Revit.
get_current_view_elements: List elements visible in the currently active view.
create_view_filter: Create, apply, or list parameter-based view filters. action=create|apply|list. A filter carries one rule (parameterName/filterRule/filterValue) or several via the rules array combined with AND/OR (logic). For apply: filterId+viewId, optional overrideR/G/B.
override_graphics: Override element graphics in a view (colors, transparency, halftone, line weight).
create_sheet: Create a new sheet in the Revit project.
place_viewport: Place a view on a sheet as a viewport, with optional rotation and viewport type.
create_schedule: Create a new schedule view in Revit.
get_schedule_data: Export schedule data as JSON from an existing schedule view.
create_preset_schedule: Create a schedule from a predefined template (e.g. RoomFinish, DoorHardware, WallQuantities, WindowSchedule).
rename_views: Batch rename views using find/replace, prefix, or suffix operations.
align_viewports: Align viewports across sheets. 'placement' matches box centers; 'model' matches the box outline min-corner so equal-scale views of the same region line up.
apply_view_template: List, apply, or remove view templates from views. action=list|apply|remove.
batch_modify_view_range: Modify view range offsets (top, cut plane, bottom, view depth) for multiple views. Offsets are in mm.
create_views_from_rooms: Create callout, section, or elevation views from rooms with a naming pattern.
manage_unplaced_views: List or delete views that are not placed on any sheet
manage_view_templates: List, duplicate, delete, or rename view templates. action=list|duplicate|delete|rename.
batch_create_sheets: Create multiple sheets with title blocks and optional view placement. sheets is a JSON array: [{number, name, titleBlockName?, viewIds?}].
create_placeholder_sheets: Create, list, convert, or delete placeholder sheets. action=create|list|convert|delete.
duplicate_sheet_with_content: Duplicate a sheet including annotations and detail items
duplicate_sheet_with_views: Duplicate a sheet N times with configurable view duplication options.
delete_schedule: Delete a schedule by ID or name
duplicate_schedule: Duplicate a schedule with a new name
